name: Algarvio Portuguese
notes: Algarvio Portuguese, the southernmost dialect of continental Portugal (Faro, Portimão, Quarteira, Lagos). Phonological features: (1) minimal unstressed vowel reduction — /e/ and /o/ are often preserved as full vowels in unstressed syllables, unlike northern EP; (2) uvular rhotic [ʁ] consistently voiced, no coda devoicing to [χ]; (3) /s/ and /z/ show apico-alveolar tendency in older and rural speakers — a residual feature linked to the Arabic (Moorish) substrate and subsequent Reconquista repopulation patterns; (4) open vowel quality: stressed [ɛ] and [ɔ] are broader than standard, reflecting southern EP vowel space; (5) vowel lengthening in stressed syllables noted in rural idiolects; (6) no retroflex consonants; (7) /lh/ [ʎ] well-preserved; (8) influence of historical Mozarabic and Arabic vocabulary audible in prosodic patterns and some lexical items. The Algarve dialect is classified as a southern EP dialect under Cintra's taxonomy; its features align it phonologically with Baixo Alentejo rather than with Brazilian Portuguese despite superficial resemblances in vowel openness.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Algarvio diverges in vowel reduction resistance, open vowel qualities, and Moorish-substrate prosodic features

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica
